fundamentals.name: Atlassian Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Atlassian Corporation provides a collaboration software that enables organizations to connect all teams through a system of work that unlocks productivity at scale worldwide. Its product portfolio includes Jira, a project management platform for planning, tracking, and managing work; Confluence, a connected workspace to create, organize, and share team knowledge, documents, and collaboration content; Loom, an asynchronous video communication tool to record and share videos; Jira Service Management, an intuitive service management solution for IT, HR, and other teams; and Rovo, an AI offering that assists teams with its Search, Chat and Agent capabilities. The company also offers Bitbucket, a git-based source code management platform for professional development teams; Compass, a developer portal that provides a unified view of engineering components; Jira Product Discovery, a tool to capture, prioritize, and roadmap product ideas; Jira Align, an enterprise agility solution that connects business and technology teams to align strategy with execution; Focus, a strategy hub for leadership teams; and Talent, a workforce planning app. In addition, it provides Trello, an AI-powered personal productivity tool; and Guard, an app for detecting and responding to security threats. The company has a strategic collaboration with Mattermost, Inc. for the development of Mattermost Docs, a sovereign, self-hosted successor to Confluence for defense, intelligence, and critical infrastructure organizations. The company was founded in 2002 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

judgment.summary: Atlassian shows strong revenue growth and positive free cash flow but struggles with profitability and has a high debt-to-equity ratio.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 31.0%; next earnings date is 2026-04-30.
positives: Revenue growth 31.7% YoY indicates strong top-line expansion.
Forward P/E of 13.76 and PEG ratio of 0.65 suggest potential undervaluation relative to future growth.
Free cash flow of $1.44B TTM is robust and indicates operational efficiency in cash generation.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target price significantly above the current spot price.
negatives: Operating margin of -2.0% and profit margin of -3.5% indicate the company is not profitable on an accounting basis.
Return on equity of -19.3% and return on assets of -1.9% show poor returns on invested capital.
Debt/Equity ratio of 141.41 is high, suggesting significant leverage.
Current ratio of 0.70 and quick ratio of 0.61 indicate potential short-term liquidity concerns.

news.paragraph: Atlassian (TEAM) is seeing analyst attention, with one report suggesting a potential 49.75% rally based on Wall Street's view. The company's last reported quarter showed a significant EPS beat, exceeding estimates by 31.0%. While the company's growth estimates remain strong, its operating and profit margins are currently negative.
